phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest crude oil supply data. Fetch and report: - OPEC+ current production policy: are cuts in place, being extended, or are production increases planned? State the exact production cut/increase level in mb/d (million barrels per day) and which countries are complying vs cheating - Any OPEC+ emergency meetings, ministerial comments, or official communiqués in the last 48 hours that signal a policy shift - US Shale / Non-OPEC output: latest EIA forecast for US crude oil production (mb/d) — is it at a record high, rising, or declining? - Baker Hughes US Oil Rig Count: latest weekly figure and the change vs prior week (rigs added or removed) — is the rig trend signalling future supply expansion or contraction? - Any other major Non-OPEC supply disruptions or ramp-ups (e.g., Libya, Nigeria, Kazakhstan, Canada) ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - OPEC+ Policy: [cut/increase/hold] | Volume: [mb/d] | Compliance: [high/low/mixed] - Key OPEC+ Comment (last 48h): [speaker + quote/summary, or None] - US Crude Production: [mb/d] | Trend: [record high / rising / flat / declining] - Baker Hughes Rig Count: [number] | Weekly Change: [+/- rigs] | Trend Signal: [supply expanding / contracting / stable] - Non-OPEC Wildcard: [country + supply impact, or None] - Supply Bias for WTI: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - SUPPLY DYNAMICS] - OPEC+ Policy: [cut/increase/hold] | Volume: [mb/d] | Compliance: [high/low/mixed] - Key OPEC+ Signal (48h): [speaker comment or "None"] - US Production: [mb/d] | Trend: [record/rising/flat/declining] - Baker Hughes Rig Count: [number] | Change: [+/- rigs] | Signal: [expanding/contracting/stable] - Non-OPEC Wildcard: [detail or "None"] - Core Logic: [how current supply posture affects WTI price direction today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): - API Weekly Crude Oil Inventory Change: Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (in million barrels — a build = bearish, a draw = bullish) - EIA Weekly Crude Oil Inventory Change: Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (in million barrels) - EIA Gasoline Inventory Change: Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (in million barrels) - EIA Distillate (Diesel/Heating Oil) Inventory Change: Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (in million barrels) - Cushing, Oklahoma crude stocks (the WTI delivery hub): latest level and weekly change — at seasonal high/low? - Any Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) release or refill actions announced by the US government this week - Overall inventory picture vs 5-year seasonal average: above or below? 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - API Crude Build/Draw: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb vs Previous mb] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - EIA Crude Build/Draw: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb vs Previous mb] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - EIA Gasoline: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb] | EIA Distillate: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb] - Cushing Stocks: [mb level] | Weekly Change: [mb] | Seasonal Position: [above/below 5yr avg] - SPR Action: [release/refill + volume, or None this week] - Inventory Bias for WTI: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - INVENTORIES & STORAGE] - API Crude: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb] | Draw/Build: [direction] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish] - EIA Crude: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb] | Draw/Build: [direction] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish] - EIA Gasoline: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb] | EIA Distillate: [Actual mb vs Forecast mb] - Cushing Stocks: [mb] | Change: [mb] | vs 5yr avg: [above/below] - SPR Action: [detail or "None"] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [how the inventory picture affects WTI price direction today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear USOIL (WTI Crude Oil) institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Supply Dynamics (OPEC+ & Non-OPEC): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Demand Outlook (Global Macro & China): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Inventories & Storage (EIA & API): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Geopolitical Risk & Disruptions: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. USD Strength & COT Sentiment: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events (e.g., EIA inventory release at 14:30 UTC Wednesday, API at 20:30 UTC Tuesday) and session liquidity.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[USOIL MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
```

phase8_blog_html: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Act as an expert Commodity Analyst and Financial Blogger. Using the research data below, write a comprehensive, highly detailed blog post explaining today's USOIL (WTI Crude Oil) fundamental bias so anyone reading it can fully understand the market conditions. Language Rule: Write the post in engaging Bengali (Bangla script), but you MUST keep all technical financial terms (e.g., Bullish, Bearish, OPEC+, mb/d, Rig Count, Inventory Draw, Inventory Build, EIA, API, SPR, COT, DXY, PMI, Geopolitical Risk Premium, Risk-On/Off, Confidence Level, Supply Disruption, Demand Destruction, Refinery Utilization) strictly in English.
